author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the same words, John i. ‘In the beginning was the word,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the same words, John i. ‘In the beginning was the word,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That our Lord Jesus Christ in seeking lost man was made Man, our preaching has never withholden, and your faith has ever retained; and moreover, that this our Lord, who for our sakes was made Man, was always God with the Father, and always will be, yea rather always Is; for where there is no succession of time, there is no “hath been” and “will be.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that of which it is said, “it hath been,” is now no more; that of which it is said, “it will be,” is not yet; but He always is, because He truly “is,” that is, is unchangeable.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Gospel lesson has just now taught us a high and divine mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this beginning of the Gospel St. John poured forth for that he drank it in from the Lord’s Breast.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye remember, that it has been very lately read to you, how that this St. John the Evangelist lay in the Lord’s Bosom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And wishing to explain this clearly, he says, “On the Lord’s Breast;” that we might understand what he meant, by “in the Lord’s bosom.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what, think we, did he drink in who was lying on the Lord’s Breast?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nay, let us not think, but drink; for we too have just now heard what we may drink in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O glorious preaching!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O the result of the full feast of the Lord’s Breast!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why seekest thou for what was before It?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Word had been made (for made indeed that was not by which all things were made); if the Word had been made, the Scripture would have said, “In the beginning God made the Word;” as it is said in Genesis, “In the beginning God made the heaven and the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God then did not in the beginning make the Word; because, “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This Word which was in the beginning, where was It?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Follow on, “And the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But from our daily hearing the words of men we are wont to think lightly of this name of “Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In this case do not think lightly of the Name of “Word;” “The Word was God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The same,” that is the Word, “was in the beginning with God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Extend your hearts, help the poverty of my words.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I shall be able to express, give ear to; on what I shall not be able to express, meditate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can comprehend the abiding Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All our words sound, and pass away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can comprehend the abiding Word, save He who abideth in Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou comprehend the abiding Word?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not follow the current of the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this flesh is indeed a current; for it has none abiding.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As it were from a kind of secret fount of nature men are born, they live, they die; or whence they come, or whither they go, we know not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a hidden water, till it issue from its source; it flows on, and is seen in its course; and again it is hidden in the sea.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us despise this stream flowing on, running, disappearing, let us despise it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All flesh is grass, and all the glory of flesh is as the flower of grass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The grass withereth, the flower falleth away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou endure?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But the word of the Lord endureth for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order to succour us, “The Word was made Flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “The Word was made Flesh”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The gold became grass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It became grass for to be burned; the grass was burned, but the gold remained; in the grass It perisheth not, yea, It changed the grass.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How did It change it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It raised it up, quickened it, lifted it up to heaven, and placed it at the right Hand of the Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But that it might be said, “And the Word was made Flesh, and dwelt among us,” let us recollect awhile what went before.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He came unto His Own, and His Own received Him not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But as many as received Him, to them gave He power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “To become,” for they “were” not; but He “was” Himself in the beginning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He gave them” then “power to become the sons of God, to them that believe in His Name; who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, born they are, in whatever age of the flesh they may be; ye see infants; see and rejoice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, they are born; but they are born of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their mother’s womb is the water of baptism.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no man in poorness of soul entertain this conceit, and turn over such most beggarly thoughts in his mind, and say to himself, “How ‘in the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God: all things were made by Him;’ and lo, ‘the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us?’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear why it was done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “To those” we know “who believed on Him He hath given power to become the sons of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not those then to whom He hath given power to become the sons of God, think it impossible to become the sons of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not imagine that it is too great a thing for you to become the sons of God; for your sakes He became the Son of man, who was the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If He was made, that He might be less, who was more; can He not bring it to pass, that of that less which we were, we may be something more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He descended to us, and shall not we ascend to Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For us He accepted our death, and shall He not give us His Life?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thee He suffered thy evil things, and shall He not give thee His good things?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But how,” one will say, “can it be, that the Word of God, by whom the world is governed, by whom all things both were, and are created, should contract Himself into the womb of a Virgin; should abandon the world, and leave the Angels, and be shut up in one woman’s womb?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou skillest not to conceive of things divine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word of God (I am speaking to thee, O man, I am speaking to thee of the omnipotence of the Word of God) could surely do all, seeing that the Word of God is omnipotent, at once remain with the Father, and come to us; at once in the flesh come forth to us, and lay concealed in Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He would not the less have been, if He had not been born of flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He “was” before His own flesh; He created His Own mother.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He chose her in whom He should be conceived, He created her of whom He should be created.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why marvellest thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is God of whom I am speaking to thee: “The Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am treating of the Word, and perchance the word of men may furnish somewhat like; though very unequal, far distant, in no way comparable, yet something which may convey a hint to you by way of resemblance.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the word which I am speaking to you, I have had previously in my heart: it came forth to thee, yet it has not departed from me; that began to be in thee, which was not in thee; it continued with me when it went forth to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As then my word was brought forth to thy sense, yet did not depart from my heart; so That Word came forth to our senses, yet departed not from His Father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: My word was with me, and it came forth into a voice: the Word of God was with the Father, and came forth into Flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But can I do with my voice that which He could do with His Flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I am not master of my voice as it flies; He is not only master of His Flesh, that It should be born, live, act; but even when dead He raised It up, and exalted unto the Father the Vehicle as it were in which He came forth to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You may call the Flesh of Christ a Garment, you may call It a Vehicle, and as perchance Himself vouchsafed to teach us, you may call It His Beast; for on this beast He raised him who had been wounded by robbers; lastly, as He said Himself more expressly, you may call It a Temple; This Temple knows death no more, Its seat is at the right Hand of the Father: in This Temple shall He come to judge the quick and dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What He hath by precept taught, He hath by example manifested.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What He hath in His own Flesh shown, that oughtest thou to hope for in thy flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is faith; hold fast what as yet thou seest not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Need there is, that by believing thou abide firm in that thou seest not; lest when thou shalt see, thou be put to shame.
